# servicenow_assignment_rule (Data Source)

`servicenow_assignment_rule` data source can be used to retrieve information of a single assignment rule in ServiceNow by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing assignment rule in ServiceNow by name.
data "servicenow_assignment_rule" "example" {
  name = "Network Incidents To Network Group"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the assignment rule.

### Read-Only

- `active` (Boolean) Whether or not this assignment rule is enabled.
- `assignment_group` (String) sys_id of the group to assign the record to.
- `condition` (String) Encoded query condition that must match for the rule to apply.
- `description` (String) Description of the assignment rule.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `match_for` (String) Match conditions setting (e.g. ALL). ServiceNow defaults this to ALL when omitted.
- `order` (Number) Order in which the rule is evaluated.
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `script` (String) Script to determine the assignment. ServiceNow seeds an example script body when this is omitted.
- `table` (String) Table that this assignment rule applies to.
- `user` (String) sys_id of the user to assign the record to.
